message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "CS-XI-U1",
      "title": "Computer Systems and Organisation Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to understanding computer architecture, software types, operating systems, logic circuits, number systems, and encoding schemes.",
      "competencies": [
        "Develop a basic understanding of computer system architecture including hardware components (CPU, memory units), input/output devices, and storage hierarchy.",
        "Explain the distinction between system software, application software, and programming tools including language translators.",
        "Describe the functions of an operating system and its user interface capabilities.",
        "Apply Boolean logic principles including truth tables, De Morgan's laws, and basic logic circuit design (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR).",
        "Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems.",
        "Identify and explain encoding schemes such as ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32) for character representation."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 1: Computer Systems and Organisation",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Systems and Organisation"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XI-U2",
      "title": "Computational Thinking and Programming Competencies (Class XI)",
      "description": "Competencies related to problem-solving methodologies, Python programming fundamentals, data types, operators, control structures, and standard library usage.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply computational thinking by analyzing problems, developing algorithms using flowcharts/pseudocode, decomposing tasks, coding, testing, and debugging.",
        "Demonstrate proficiency in the Python language including execution modes (interactive/script), character sets, tokens, variables, l-value/r-value concepts, and comments.",
        "Differentiate between mutable and immutable data types including Numbers, Booleans, Strings, Lists, Tuples, Dictionaries, and None.",
        "Utilize various operators (arithmetic, relational, logical, assignment, identity, membership) with correct precedence rules for expression evaluation.",
        "Implement control flow structures using conditional statements (if-else, if-elif-else) and iterative loops (for, while), including nested loops and break/continue statements.",
        "Manipulate strings and sequences performing operations like concatenation, slicing, traversal, and utilizing built-in methods for case conversion, counting, searching, etc.",
        "Manage data structures by implementing list, tuple, and dictionary operations such as indexing, appending, extending, sorting, filtering, and nested structure handling.",
        "Identify and handle different types of errors: syntax, logical, and run-time errors."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I",
      "chapter_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming - I"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XI-U3",
      "title": "Society, Law, and Ethics Competencies (Class XI)",
      "description": "Competencies related to digital citizenship, legal frameworks, cyber safety, ethical considerations in technology use.",
      "competencies": [
        "Adhere to net etiquettes and communication protocols on social media platforms.",
        "Understand Intellectual Property Rights including copyright, patents, trademarks, open source licensing (GPL, Apache), and identify violations like plagiarism.",
        "Recognize various cyber crimes such as hacking, phishing, ransomware, eavesdropping, cyber bullying, and fraud.",
        "Implement cyber safety practices including identity protection, confidentiality measures, and safe web browsing habits.",
        "Identify types of malware (viruses, trojans, adware) and their impacts on systems.",
        "Understand the importance of E-waste management regarding proper disposal of electronic gadgets.",
        "Analyze gender and disability issues in the context of teaching and using computers."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit 3: Society, Law and Ethics",
      "chapter_name": "Society, Law and Ethics"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U1",
      "title": "Advanced Programming Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to advanced Python concepts including functions, exception handling, file operations, and data structures.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply the concept of functions by defining user-defined functions with arguments, parameters (positional/default), return values, scope management (global/local), and flow execution."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2",
      "chapter_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U1-FileHandling",
      "title": "File Handling Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to reading, writing, and manipulating text, binary, and CSV files.",
      "competencies": [
        "Perform file operations on Text Files including opening modes (r, w, a), using context managers ('with' clause), read/write methods, seek/tell positioning, and data manipulation."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2",
      "chapter_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U1-BinaryCSV",
      "title": "Binary and CSV File Handling Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to handling binary files using pickle module and managing CSV data.",
      "competencies": [
        "Execute basic operations on Binary Files including open modes, close methods, importing the pickle module for dump/load operations, search, append, update, and read/write functionalities."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2",
      "chapter_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U1-Stacks",
      "title": "Data Structures Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to implementing stack data structures.",
      "competencies": [
        "Implement the Stack data structure using lists, performing push and pop operations."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2",
      "chapter_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U1-ExceptionHandling",
      "title": "Exception Handling Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to error management in Python programs.",
      "competencies": [
        "Handle exceptions using try-except-finally blocks to manage runtime errors gracefully."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2",
      "chapter_name": "Computational Thinking and Programming – 2"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U2",
      "title": "Computer Networks Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to networking evolution, communication models, media, devices, topologies, protocols, and web services.",
      "competencies": [
        "Trace the evolution of computer networks from ARPANET/NSFNET to the INTERNET."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computer Networks",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Networks"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U2-CommModels",
      "title": "Data Communication Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to data communication components and metrics.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the concept of data communication including sender, receiver, message, media, protocols, bandwidth, transfer rates, IP addressing, and switching techniques."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computer Networks",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Networks"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U2-MediaDevices",
      "title": "Transmission Media and Devices Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to physical media types and network hardware.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate between wired transmission media (Twisted pair, Co-axial, Fiber-optic) and wireless media (Radio waves, Microwaves, Infrared)."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computer Networks",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Networks"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U2-NetworkTypes",
      "title": "Network Topologies and Types Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to network classifications and physical/logical layouts.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN) and topologies (Bus, Star, Tree)."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computer Networks",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Networks"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U2-ProtocolsWeb",
      "title": "Network Protocols and Web Services Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to communication protocols and web infrastructure.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain network protocols including HTTP, FTP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computer Networks",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Networks"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U2-WebServices",
      "title": "Introduction to Web Services Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to web technologies and infrastructure.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the components of web services including WWW, HTML, XML, domain names, URLs, websites, browsers, servers, and hosting."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Computer Networks",
      "chapter_name": "Computer Networks"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-DBConcepts",
      "title": "Database Concepts Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to fundamental database theory and relational models.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the need for databases and core concepts including relations, attributes, tuples, domains."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-RelationalModel",
      "title": "Relational Data Model Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to keys and relational properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define degree, cardinality, and various types of keys: candidate key, primary key, alternate key, foreign key."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-SQLDDL_DML",
      "title": "SQL Data Definition and Manipulation Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to creating, altering, dropping database objects and managing data.",
      "competencies": [
        "Execute SQL commands for Database Management: create/use/drop databases, show tables."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-SQL_TableOps",
      "title": "SQL Table Operations Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to table structure modification and data manipulation.",
      "competencies": [
        "Perform Data Definition Language operations: create/describe/alter tables, add/remove attributes or primary keys."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-SQL_Querying",
      "title": "SQL Querying and Filtering Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to retrieving data using SELECT statements.",
      "competencies": [
        "Execute Data Manipulation Language queries: insert, delete, select with operators, aliasing, distinct clause."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-SQL_AdvancedQuery",
      "title": "Advanced SQL Query Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to complex querying including filtering, sorting, grouping, and joining.",
      "competencies": [
        "Construct advanced queries using where clause, in/between operators, order by, is null/is not null, like operator."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-SQL_AggregateJoin",
      "title": "SQL Aggregation and Joins Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to aggregate functions and table joins.",
      "competencies": [
        "Utilize aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count) with group by and having clauses."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-PythonSQL",
      "title": "Python-SQL Interface Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to integrating Python with SQL databases.",
      "competencies": [
        "Establish database connectivity using connect(), cursor() methods."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-PythonSQL_Ops",
      "title": "Python-SQL Operations Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to executing queries and handling results in Python.",
      "competencies": [
        "Perform insert, update, delete operations using cursor.execute() with format specifiers (%s or format())."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    },
    {
      "code": "CS-XII-U3-PythonSQL_Display",
      "title": "Python-SQL Data Retrieval Competencies (Class XII)",
      "description": "Competencies related to fetching and displaying data from SQL databases via Python.",
      "competencies": [
        "Display retrieved data using fetchone(), fetchall() methods."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Database Management",
      "chapter_name": "Database Management"
    }
  ]
}
